title: hydrangea japonica var. rosea
intro: Hydrangea japonica var. rosea, also known as the pink hydrangea, is a deciduous shrub native to Japan and China, characterized by its large, pink, globe-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of hydrangea japonica var. rosea include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting urinary health, making it a popular remedy for conditions like arthritis and kidney stones. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional.
